From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Royal ceremonial hall in Brunei

For the former building, see [Old Lapau](/wiki/Old_Lapau "Old Lapau").

| Lapau | |

|  | |

| General information | |

| [Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system") | [4°53′33″N 114°56′34″E﻿ / ﻿4.89243612°N 114.94288064°E﻿ / 4.89243612; 114.94288064](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Lapau&params=4.89243612_N_114.94288064_E_type:landmark_region:BN) |

The **Lapau**, also known as the **Royal Ceremonial Hall**,[[1]](#cite_note-commonwealthwalkway-1) is a ceremonial hall in [Bandar Seri Begawan](/wiki/Bandar_Seri_Begawan "Bandar Seri Begawan"), [Brunei](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei"). It is where the royal ceremonies, [state investiture](/wiki/Orders,_decorations,_and_medals_of_Brunei "Orders, decorations, and medals of Brunei") and some state events are traditionally held. It was the place where the present [Sultan of Brunei](/wiki/Sultan_of_Brunei "Sultan of Brunei"), [Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah](/wiki/Sultan_Hassanal_Bolkiah "Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah"), was crowned in 1968.

## Name

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lapau&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Name")]

*Lapau* is historically the type of a Bruneian traditional hall where royal ceremonies and audiences were held. It is believed to have existed since the first [Sultan of Brunei](/wiki/Sultan_of_Brunei "Sultan of Brunei"), [Sultan Muhammad Shah](/wiki/Muhammad_Shah_of_Brunei "Muhammad Shah of Brunei"), and since then various *lapau* buildings had been built over the course of time.[[2]](#cite_note-brudirect_18.5.2017-2) Lapau Kajang was the *lapau* which held the silver jubilee celebration of the ascension to the throne of [Sultan Ahmad Tajuddin](/wiki/Sultan_Ahmad_Tajuddin "Sultan Ahmad Tajuddin") as the 28th Sultan on 22 September 1949.[[2]](#cite_note-brudirect_18.5.2017-2)

## Location

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lapau&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Location")]

The building is located in [Pusat Bandar](/wiki/Pusat_Bandar,_Brunei "Pusat Bandar, Brunei"), the [city centre](/wiki/City_centre "City centre") of Bandar Seri Begawan.[[3]](#cite_note-street_directory-3) It is part of the complex which also houses the *Dewan Majlis*, the former building of the [Legislative Council](/wiki/Legislative_Council_of_Brunei "Legislative Council of Brunei").[[1]](#cite_note-commonwealthwalkway-1) Some notable landmarks in its vicinity include the [Royal Regalia Museum](/wiki/Royal_Regalia_Museum "Royal Regalia Museum"), the [Youth Centre](/wiki/Bandar_Seri_Begawan#Youth_centre "Bandar Seri Begawan") and the Main Post Office (*Pejabat Besar Pos*) building.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lapau&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: History")]

Prior to the existence of the present building, the function of *lapau* was housed in another building which was also known as Lapau. It was built during the reign of [Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III](/wiki/Sultan_Omar_Ali_Saifuddien_III "Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III") and still exists today, whereby it is now commonly known as the [Old Lapau](/wiki/Old_Lapau "Old Lapau") (*Lapau Lama*) to distinguish from the current Lapau.[[4]](#cite_note-Bangunan_Lapau-4) It is now part of the [Brunei History Centre](/wiki/Brunei_History_Centre "Brunei History Centre") building.[[4]](#cite_note-Bangunan_Lapau-4) Since 2018 it has been repurposed into a historical gallery pertaining to the [Constitution of Brunei](/wiki/Constitution_of_Brunei "Constitution of Brunei").[[5]](#cite_note-Salawati_24.12.2018-5)

The plan to build a new *lapau* building was first proposed in 1963.[[4]](#cite_note-Bangunan_Lapau-4) The plan as well as the design of the new building was first proposed by [Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III](/wiki/Sultan_Omar_Ali_Saifuddien_III "Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III") himself, and later refined by the commissioned architect firm [Booty Edwards & Partners](/wiki/Booty_Edwards_%26_Partners "Booty Edwards & Partners").[[4]](#cite_note-Bangunan_Lapau-4) It was built at a site which was then in [Kampong Kianggeh](/wiki/Kampong_Kianggeh "Kampong Kianggeh").[[4]](#cite_note-Bangunan_Lapau-4) The construction began in August 1965 and completed in 1968.[[4]](#cite_note-Bangunan_Lapau-4)

The present *lapau* was where [Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah](/wiki/Sultan_Hassanal_Bolkiah "Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah") was crowned and bestowed with the title *Sultan dan Yang Di-Pertuan* in a coronation ceremony known as *Istiadat Berpuspa* or *Istiadat Perpuspaan* on 1 August 1968, nearly 10 months after he was officially declared as the 29th Sultan of Brunei upon the abdication of [Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III](/wiki/Sultan_Omar_Ali_Saifuddien_III "Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III").[[6]](#cite_note-Fatimah_4.1.2017-6) It was also the place where the agreement was signed between Brunei and the Great Britain on 23 November 1971, which gave Brunei full internal independence except defence and foreign affairs.[[2]](#cite_note-brudirect_18.5.2017-2)

## Ceremonies

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lapau&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Ceremonies")]

The Lapau is the traditional place for the 'opening' ceremonies of the *[Gendang Jaga-Jaga](/w/index.php?title=Gendang_Jaga-Jaga&action=edit&redlink=1 "Gendang Jaga-Jaga (page does not exist)") ensemble*, a Bruneian traditional act to mark the start of a royal event or festivity such as coronations, the anniversary of the ascension to the throne, birthdays, and royal weddings.[[7]](#cite_note-Hakim_20.1.2022-7) The *Gendang Jaga-Jaga* itself is a Bruneian traditional [musical ensemble](/wiki/Musical_ensemble "Musical ensemble") which is only played every day during seasons of royal events as specified above.[[7]](#cite_note-Hakim_20.1.2022-7)

The Lapau is also where the 'opening' ceremony of the *Naubat* is held. This is a traditional ceremony, carried out to mark the end of the mourning period upon the demise of the Sultan or a royal family member.[[8]](#cite_note-Ak_Jefferi_15.7.2020-8) The *Naubat* has the same musical instruments as the *Gendang Jaga-Jaga*, with the exception of the gongs (two standard and one nipple known as a *canang*), which are only used in the Gendang Jaga-Jaga ensemble.[[8]](#cite_note-Ak_Jefferi_15.7.2020-8) Today, the *Naubat* is performed daily throughout the life of a reigning Sultan before the dawn, sunset and evening prayers.[[8]](#cite_note-Ak_Jefferi_15.7.2020-8)

Royal ceremonies have been traditionally held in the Lapau. However, nowadays and due to capacity constraint they are held in [Istana Nurul Iman](/wiki/Istana_Nurul_Iman "Istana Nurul Iman").[[4]](#cite_note-Bangunan_Lapau-4) Only public royal ceremonies like the openings of the *Naubat* and the *Gendang Jaga-Jaga* are still held in the Lapau.
